generated_comment:
Divya: Honestly, I don’t get why people make such a big issue of those common gaalis in office banter. Half the time it’s just frustration, not like anyone is literally insulting somebody’s mother or sister. 
Husband: Hmm.
Divya: And when someone says a guy is behaving like a girl or asks him to toughen up, it’s rude maybe, but yaar people need to stop acting like every word has some deep sexist meaning.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/family-based abusive language as 'just expression' and dismisses concern about feminizing men as insult. It encodes the frame’s defensiveness ('it’s just a word') and the minimization of sexist structure in everyday gaalis.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Divya. Age 35. A senior software engineer in Bengaluru. You are married with one child. You are venting to your husband at home after dinner. Your skip level manager pulled you into a meeting and told you that if you plan to have another child you should think carefully about whether this is the right time to take on a high visibility project. You are telling your husband what was said to you.
